login.subtitle: Sign in to continue
login.tagline: The wizard who triages your pull requests
secrets.help.title: How to create a GitHub token
secrets.help.intro: wshm uses a GitHub token to read issues / PRs and (optionally) post comments and labels. Without one, anonymous mode caps you at 60 req/h and read-only public repos.
secrets.help.step1: Open github.com/settings/tokens and click "Generate new token (classic)".
secrets.help.step2: Pick a name (e.g. "wshm-cloud") and an expiration (90 days recommended).
secrets.help.step3: Scopes: leave empty for public-repo read-only (5 000 req/h, can't comment); pick "public_repo" if you want wshm to label / comment.
secrets.help.step4: Click Generate, copy the token ("ghp_…"), paste it here as Value with key "github_token".
secrets.help.tip: Already logged in via gh CLI? Run "gh auth token" and paste the output. Note: that token is your personal one — wshm actions will appear as you.
settings.features.mode.body.apply: wshm will post comments, set labels, and open PRs based on the AI actions enabled below.
settings.features.mode.body.dryrun: wshm computes AI classifications visible in the Triage / PRs tabs but does not write to GitHub. Toggle on to start applying actions.
settings.features.mode.tip: Apply mode posts to GitHub: comments, labels, opened PRs, merges. Dry-run computes the same AI results but only stores them locally for review in the dashboard. Toggle takes effect on the next sync — no daemon restart.
settings.features.collection.title: Read-only collection
settings.features.collection.body: Sync GitHub data into the local DB. Always safe — no writes back.
settings.features.collection.issues.help: — sync open + closed issues to local DB
settings.features.collection.issues.tip: Pulls every open + recently-closed issue from GitHub into the local SQLite DB on each scheduled sync. Required for the Issues tab and for triage. Disabling stops the sync but keeps already-collected issues in the DB.
settings.features.collection.prs.tip: Pulls open PRs along with their CI status, mergeability, labels, and authors. Required for the PRs tab, the Queue (merge ranking), and PR analysis.
settings.features.ai.title: AI classification
settings.features.ai.body: When Mode: Apply is on, these run on every sync and post results to GitHub. In dry-run they still run but only update the local DB and the dashboard tabs.
settings.features.ai.dimmed: (computed only — no GitHub writes while in dry-run)
settings.features.ai.triage.help: — AI category + priority; posts labels and a summary comment when Apply is on
settings.features.ai.triage.tip: Sends each new issue title + body to your AI provider. Returns a category (bug/feature/question/...), priority (low/medium/high/critical), and confidence score (0-100%). When Apply is on, also posts the suggested labels and a one-line summary comment. Skipped for issues matching the triage_skip_labels filter below.
settings.features.ai.analyze.help: — AI risk + type + checklist; posts a summary comment when Apply is on
settings.features.ai.analyze.tip: Sends the PR diff + body to your AI provider and gets back a risk level (low/medium/high), PR type (feature/fix/refactor/...), summary, and a review checklist. When Apply is on, posts a summary comment on the PR. Skipped for drafts when skip_drafts is enabled.
settings.features.ai.review.help: — inline code review on PR diffs (Pro feature)
settings.features.ai.review.tip: Pro feature. Sends the PR diff to the AI for line-by-line review. When Apply is on, posts inline review comments on suspicious lines (security, perf, bugs, style). Higher token cost than Analyze PRs — use it on PRs that matter.
settings.features.auto.title: Auto-actions
settings.features.auto.body: Mutating actions on the repo. Off by default; require Mode: Apply to take effect.
settings.features.auto.fix.help: — open PRs for simple bugs flagged by triage
settings.features.auto.fix.tip: For issues triage flags as is_simple_fix=true with high confidence, ask the AI to generate a patch and open a PR linked to the issue. Restricted to the auto_pr_only_labels filter below (default: "good first issue", "help wanted") to avoid touching anything risky.
settings.features.auto.merge.help: — merge ready PRs once checks pass + queue score above threshold
settings.features.auto.merge.tip: For PRs that pass the queue scoring threshold (configured in [queue].merge_threshold) and meet the auto_merge_* filters (only_labels, only_authors, max_loc), perform the merge using your configured strategy (rebase by default). Conservative defaults: max 200 LoC, dependabot[bot] only, label-gated.
